scope_refinement:
TrueScan was originally scoped as a multimodal (visual + audio) deepfake
detection system. During Phase 3 the active project team changed from
three members to two. To preserve implementation depth, reproducibility,
and evaluation rigor for the remaining two members, we refined the scope
to image-level visual deepfake detection only. Audio detection (ASVspoof,
WaveFake, AASIST, LFCC features) and video-level temporal aggregation
are deferred to future work.
future_work: multimodal fusion of audio and visual signals

primary_metrics.F1: reported at threshold=0.50 AND at the val-tuned optimal threshold
primary_metrics.PR-AUC: reported when there is meaningful class imbalance
primary_metrics.confusion_matrix: plotted for FF++ test and Celeb-DF

evaluation_levels.image_level: all metrics computed per face crop (no video-level voting)

split_strategy.calibration_held_out: 10% of val held out exclusively for temperature scaling
split_strategy.celebdf: external zero-shot test only; never used for training or tuning

calibration_and_abstention.temperature_scaling: fit T via LBFGS on the held-out calibration split
calibration_and_abstention.metrics: ECE (15 bins) and Brier score before/after

robustness.reporting: AUROC and F1 deltas vs clean; visualized as a bar chart

imbalance_handling: WeightedRandomSampler + Focal+BCE blend; per-class metrics always reported
leakage_awareness: identity-level splits + assertion check; Celeb-DF kept separate
